description_long: This object consists of several rifle cartridges and a metallic magazine, arranged in a scattered manner. The cartridges are brass-colored with pointed copper tips, typical of rifle ammunition, and are shown both loose and partially loaded into the magazine. The magazine itself is curved and metallic, with a dark gray finish and visible seams along its body. The bullets are cylindrical with a tapered tip, and the primer ends are visible on some of the loose rounds. The overall appearance suggests these are centerfire rifle rounds, likely for a military-style rifle. The materials and details indicate a utilitarian, robust design suitable for urban or tactical environments.
description: A group of brass rifle cartridges and a curved metallic magazine, with bullets both loose and partially loaded. The arrangement highlights the robust, utilitarian design of the ammunition and magazine.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the magazine lying on its side with several cartridges scattered around, some standing upright and others lying flat.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the magazine from a slightly rear angle, emphasizing the open end and the alignment of cartridges inside and outside the magazine.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the bullets and magazine from above, highlighting the pointed tips and the arrangement of loose rounds.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a perspective where the magazine and bullets are seen from the opposite side, with the primer ends of some cartridges visible.
